meta.epistemic_contract: FACT cited · INFERENCE labelled · UNKNOWN/STALE aloud · nothing is a trade until the harness clears it

dimensions.name: pit_lag_proof
dimensions.detail: no unbacked lag-0 claims; daily defaults fail closed

members.proposed_relationship: Test whether |basis| percentile (ChinaA 10.2% vs cross-country median ~<1%) predicts subsequent narrowing between T2 return_21d and ASHR return_21d
Test whether ChinaA's negative equity_state return_21d_z (-1.70) co-moves with ChinaH return_21d_z (-1.55) but decouples from ASHR local performance
Test whether FX_VOL_TERM_Z252 (+1.02) and FX_RR25_Z252 (+1.09) for CNY provide a leading channel for onshore/offshore equity gap

fatal_if: The 10.2% basis is a data artifact (stale ASHR mark, mismatched currency conversion, or index-methodology mismatch between T2 synthetic and ASHR NAV) rather than a tradable dislocation
T2 return series embeds forward-looking rebalancing or uses a different as-of timestamp than ASHR daily prices (temporal leakage)
must_check: Reconcile T2 construction methodology and timestamp against ASHR NAV/price source for 2026-06-30
Confirm basis is persistently large over prior 20 sessions, not a single-day print
Verify capital-control / accessibility constraints that structurally prevent basis arbitrage before treating gap as signal

self_falsification.strongest_counterargument: A 10.2% onshore-offshore basis for China is often structural (capital controls, quota limits, currency wedge) and can persist indefinitely without convergence, so the 'dislocation' may be permanent regime not a mispricing.
what_would_change_my_mind: Evidence the basis mean-reverts historically within a definable horizon
Discovery that T2 and ASHR track the same underlying basket with identical timestamps, making 10.2% an error not a feature

discovery_lab.title: Extreme valuation vs weak momentum cluster
discovery_lab.subtitle: 3 proposed members

members.proposed_relationship: Test whether countries in top-decile CAPE_PCTILE_10Y AND negative return_21d_z exhibit different forward dispersion than either signal alone (nonlinear interaction)
Test whether Canada's FX_RR25_Z252 (+1.82) and FX_VOL_TERM_Z252 (+0.85) — elevated vs peers — flag building downside skew demand alongside its 100th-pct CAPE
Test whether Korea's massive return_63d (+0.69) combined with negative return_21d_z (-1.25) marks a momentum-exhaustion inflection

fatal_if: CAPE/PB percentiles are computed only over a 10Y window that mechanically pins recent-high markets to 99-100 (window artifact, not true richness)
return_21d_z normalization uses a lookback overlapping the return window, creating spurious negative-z at highs (construction leakage)
must_check: Confirm valuation percentile denominators and that 99-100 prints reflect real distribution tails
Check whether the valuation-momentum interaction adds information beyond each marginal signal
Verify Japan's return_21d ~0.0% but z -0.54 is consistent (vol scaling sanity)

self_falsification.strongest_counterargument: High valuation with recent weakness is the single most common and well-arbitraged setup; 'expensive and falling' rarely carries residual edge after controls for country, sector, and known value/momentum factors.
what_would_change_my_mind: The nonlinear valuation×momentum interaction shows no incremental signal over additive factors
Percentiles collapse to non-extreme under a longer or expanding-window valuation baseline

members.proposed_relationship: Test whether extreme positive flow_z INTO negative price momentum (Indonesia) vs INTO positive momentum (Italy) predict divergent forward behavior (flow-fighting-tape vs flow-confirming-tape)
Test whether Indonesia's high ETF_SHORT_PCT_Z (+2.71) co-occurring with high inflow flags creation/redemption mechanics rather than directional conviction
Test whether Indonesia's elevated FX_IMPVOL_Z252 (+1.26) and FX_VOL_TERM_Z252 (+1.16) reinforce a stress-flow interpretation vs Italy's benign FX state

fatal_if: ETF_FLOW_21D_Z as of 2026-06-30 incorporates flows dated after the price snapshot (look-ahead in flow aggregation)
Extreme flow_z values (+3.2) are outliers driven by a single primary-market creation unrelated to secondary sentiment (artifact)
must_check: Confirm flow window end date <= price as_of with no forward contamination
Verify flow_z normalization sample size for small ETFs (EIDO liquidity 'medium') is adequate
Distinguish inflow driven by short-covering vs new long demand using ETF_SHORT_PCT_Z jointly

self_falsification.strongest_counterargument: ETF flows into single-country funds are frequently noise or mechanical (index rebalance, model portfolios) and 'inflow into falling market' can simply be dip-buying that keeps losing; sign of flow×momentum interaction may be unstable out of sample.
what_would_change_my_mind: Flow×momentum interaction term shows no stable conditional structure across countries
Indonesia's +3.21 flow_z traces to one creation event, invalidating the sentiment reading

discovery_lab.title: Sovereign curve stress under quiet FX
discovery_lab.subtitle: 3 proposed members

members.proposed_relationship: Test whether extreme negative SOV_2S10S_Z252 (>|2|) precedes changes in equity return_21d_z when FX_IMPVOL_Z252 is still low/negative (rates-leads-equity)
Test whether Indonesia's simultaneous curve stress (-2.24), high CDS slope (52bp), and elevated FX vol form a coherent sovereign-stress cluster distinct from DM flatteners (Denmark/Germany)
Test whether Denmark's -2.26 flattening paired with bottom-decile valuation (CAPE pctile 4.2) is a benign vs stress signature

fatal_if: SOV_2S10S_Z252 derived series uses a 252d window during an atypical rate regime producing mechanical extreme z without economic content
Denmark liquidity 'micro' and sparse FX data mean its sovereign signal is unreliable/stale (artifact)
must_check: Verify curve z-scores against raw 2s10s levels to confirm real flattening magnitude
Check whether DM vs EM flatteners need separate baselines
Confirm SOV_CDS_SLOPE_BP and 2s10s_z tell a consistent story per country before clustering

self_falsification.strongest_counterargument: Curve flattening is a macro-slow variable; equity ETFs may already discount it, and cross-country z-scores conflate very different rate regimes (Denmark peg, Indonesia EM, Japan steepening) into a spurious common factor.
what_would_change_my_mind: Rates-leads-equity relationship shows no lead-lag once regime-controlled
Extreme z-scores disappear under level-based (non-z) curve measures

members.proposed_relationship: Test whether Chile's -4.2% basis (second-widest magnitude after ChinaA) predicts subsequent T2/ECH convergence direction
Test whether extreme FX_RR25_Z252 (+2.14, most positive in snapshot) signals CLP risk-reversal demand that explains the USD-ETF vs local-T2 gap
Test whether medium liquidity + stale ETF_SHORT_PCT_Z (dated 2017) degrades reliability of Chile's flow state relative to price-derived state

fatal_if: ECH price on 2026-06-30 is stale or reflects a large CLP move not captured in T2 (currency-translation artifact producing fake basis)
Chile ECO_INFL_SURPRISE_Z (dated 2019) and ETF_SHORT (dated 2017) indicate a broadly stale data record making the whole country snapshot unreliable
must_check: Confirm ECH NAV vs price and CLP fixing timestamps against T2 as-of
Flag and down-weight all Chile signals with stale dates before use
Verify basis sign convention (T2 minus ETF) is consistent across countries

self_falsification.strongest_counterargument: Chile's gap is most likely a currency-translation and stale-data artifact (multiple fields years old), so the apparent dislocation is a data-hygiene problem, not a signal.
what_would_change_my_mind: Timestamps reconcile and the -4.2% basis persists with fresh CLP fixings
Basis shows historical mean-reversion for Chile specifically rather than random drift

members.proposed_relationship: Sign(return_21d T2) != Sign(ETF 21d return) with |basis| in top decile cross-sectionally (ChinaA basis 10.2% vs peers <1.5%)
|basis| magnitude ranked cross-sectionally vs subsequent basis compression toward 0
ChinaA vs ChinaH divergence: ChinaA basis 10.2% but ChinaH basis 0.6% despite near-identical sovereign/FX state — tests whether the dislocation is A-share-share-class specific

fatal_if: The 10.2% basis is a units/currency artifact or stale T2 proxy (CNY vs USD ETF denomination) rather than a real tradable dislocation
T2 return_21d and ASHR return are computed over misaligned windows (holiday calendar mismatch inflates apparent basis)
must_check: Confirm T2 proxy and ASHR NAV are in the same currency and same 21d calendar window
Check historical distribution of ChinaA basis for structural persistence (onshore/offshore capital controls may make 10% basis a permanent feature, not a signal)
Verify ASHR premium/discount to NAV separately from the T2 proxy construction

self_falsification.strongest_counterargument: China A-shares are structurally segmented by capital controls; a large A-vs-offshore basis can persist indefinitely and is not mean-reverting on any tradable horizon, so the 'dislocation' is a feature of the market microstructure, not mispricing.
what_would_change_my_mind: Evidence that historical ChinaA basis of this magnitude reliably compressed within a defined horizon
Confirmation the basis is not a currency-denomination or calendar artifact

members.proposed_relationship: ETF_FLOW_21D_Z > +3 combined with VAL_PB_PCTILE_10Y > 95 (Italy) — tests exhaustion of inflow into rich valuation
ETF_FLOW_21D_Z > +3 combined with return_21d < -8% and VAL_PB_PCTILE_10Y < 5 (Indonesia) — tests dip-buying flow into a crashing cheap market
Cross-sectional interaction: sign(flow z) x valuation percentile as a joint state vs subsequent return dispersion

fatal_if: ETF_FLOW_21D_Z for micro/low-liquidity ETFs is dominated by single-creation-unit noise (Indonesia EIDO, Italy EWI liquidity 'medium') making z-scores non-informative
Flow z and short z are mechanically correlated (same AP creation/redemption double-counted)
must_check: Normalize flow z by ETF AUM and typical creation-unit size to rule out single-print artifacts
Check whether valuation percentile null-handling (Indonesia CAPE is null) biases the pairing
Confirm flow z window (21d) does not overlap the return window in a way that induces mechanical anticorrelation

self_falsification.strongest_counterargument: Extreme flow z-scores in medium/low-liquidity single-country ETFs are frequently just lumpy institutional rebalancing with no predictive content; the valuation pairing is post-hoc pattern selection across 20 countries where extremes appear by chance.
what_would_change_my_mind: Flow z survives AUM normalization and remains extreme
A pre-registered cross-sectional test shows flow x valuation interaction has stable sign out-of-sample

members.proposed_relationship: SOV_2S10S_Z252 < -1.8 while SOV_2S10S_PCT still > 0 — tests rapid flattening as a leading macro-regime signal vs equity return_21d_z
Indonesia uniquely already inverted (slope -0.048) with z -2.24 AND equity return_21d -9.0% — tests whether inversion+crash cluster differs from flatten-only cluster
Joint state of SOV_2S10S_Z252 and CONS_GDP_REV3M_12M (Germany/Italy/Denmark all show negative or flat GDP revisions) as a combined deterioration detector

fatal_if: 252d z-score of 2s10s is dominated by a single policy-rate step (level shift) making z mechanically extreme without economic meaning
Sovereign curve series for Eurozone members (Germany/Italy/France) are the same underlying and thus not independent observations
must_check: Decompose 2s10s move into front-end (policy) vs long-end (term premium) contribution
Verify cross-country curve series are independent, not a shared benchmark
Check whether curve z has any historical lead/lag relationship to equity state in-sample before claiming predictive value

self_falsification.strongest_counterargument: Curve flattening z-scores this large are usually a coincident reflection of already-known central-bank paths; equities have long since discounted the flattening, so there is no residual unpriced information.
what_would_change_my_mind: Evidence the equity market did NOT move contemporaneously with the curve flattening (residual gap exists)
Historical demonstration curve-z leads equity-state changes rather than lagging

members.proposed_relationship: FX_RR25_Z252 cross-sectional extremes (Chile +2.14, Canada +1.82, Brazil -1.42) vs subsequent FX and local-equity co-movement
Japan BF25_Z252 +2.55 (tail-hedging demand) with RR near neutral (-0.48) — tests isolated butterfly spike as a tail-risk-repricing precursor
Divergence between FX_IMPVOL_Z252 and FX_BF25_Z252 sign (e.g., low impvol but high BF) as a mispriced-tail detector

fatal_if: FX option z-scores are shared/duplicated across Eurozone members (France/Germany/Italy identical RR +0.75) indicating a single EUR series mis-attributed per country
Derived FX vol surfaces are stale or interpolated, making 252d z unreliable at the tails
must_check: Confirm each country's FX pair is distinct and RR/BF are natively quoted, not proxied from EUR/USD
Check option liquidity and quote freshness for Chile/Brazil (EM FX vol often thin)
Validate that z252 normalization window contains enough non-stale observations

self_falsification.strongest_counterargument: FX risk-reversal extremes primarily forecast FX, not local equity returns; using them as an equity regime-flip detector conflates two distinct risk premia and the linkage may be spurious.
what_would_change_my_mind: Demonstrated in-sample lead from FX skew z to local equity state changes
Confirmation FX series are country-native and not EUR duplicates

members.proposed_relationship: return_63d top-decile AND return_21d_z < -1 (deceleration) AND VAL_CAPE_PCTILE_10Y = 100 — tests momentum exhaustion at valuation ceiling
ETF_SHORT_PCT_Z rising (+2.12) alongside recent negative 21d return — tests whether short build-up confirms or fades the reversal
Korea basis +1.9% (EWY vs T2 gap) as a secondary confirmation of proxy/ETF divergence

fatal_if: The +68.9% 63d return is a data error, corporate action, or index-reconstitution artifact
return_21d_z normalization is distorted by the same outlier run that produced the 63d spike
must_check: Verify the 63d return against the underlying KOSPI/EWY price series for splits or reconstitution
Confirm valuation percentiles are current-month and not carried forward
Check short-interest z freshness (dated 2026-06-15) vs price as-of 2026-06-30

self_falsification.strongest_counterargument: Strong 63d momentum with high valuation is the classic 'expensive winners keep winning' regime; the 21d dip may be routine profit-taking within an intact uptrend, and shorting valuation extremes has historically bled money.
what_would_change_my_mind: Evidence the momentum has structurally broken (breadth/flow deterioration beyond a single 21d window)
Confirmation the 63d return is genuine and not an artifact

holdout.note: Forward holdout is accumulating; backfill is a plumbing smoke test.

combiner.ic_series_note: No combiner IC time series is persisted in the loop DB; report cross-sectional scores only until one is built.

families.label: Ridge combiner
families.note: In-sample-selected components (2026-06); a ceiling, not proof. Shown as the LEAN column, never counted as an independent vote.

families.label: Lead-lag 5d gap
families.note: Structural followers only (Asia+Europe); U.S. leads but is never led.

families.label: PIT two-hop trade gap
families.note: Point-in-time edge vintages; survived the PIT re-test.

families.label: PIT bank-neighbor gap
families.note: BIS LBS banking-exposure neighbors; PIT vintages.

families.label: Fundamental-twin divergence
families.note: Top-5 cosine fundamental twins (month-end factor vectors, PIT).

families.label: ECFC CPI revision (reflation)
families.note: Reflation lens: upward CPI consensus revisions -> relative outperformance. Monthly cadence — as-of lags the daily columns.

families.label: ETF-flow contrarian
families.note: FLIPPED registration: stretched 21d inflows mark dumb-money chasing -> underperformance. IC/NW-t shown in the registered (contrarian) direction.

families.label: ToT impulse z
families.note: D1's terms-of-trade impulse (Comtrade net shares x Pink Sheet 3m returns, z vs 36m) WITHOUT the equity/REER quiet gates. Context column — no harness verdict exists.

consensus.voting_note: Agreement counts exclude the combiner (outcome-trained on the other columns) and the untested ToT column.

connections.title: Netherlands melt-up sits atop a -3.73σ inflation miss and its own dead signal ranks
connections.mechanism: Netherlands is +11.82% 1m (best in universe) and D2 flags it short at -2.77 (intensifying, 5d) for outrunning neighbors. Yet it is dead-last in graph_bank (21/21), graph_twohop (30/30), leadlag (15/15) and twins 33/34, while its ECO_INFL/CPI_SURPRISE_Z is -3.73 — the second-worst inflation miss in the packet. Its largest trade partner Germany (NLD-DEU trade ~359.5bn) also posted -3.87σ inflation misses and is -3.27% 1m. If the rally were disinflation-driven re-rating, Germany should confirm; it does not. Bonus twist: Netherlands is a lead-lag leader of Taiwan (0.404), which itself carries a new D9 flag (-2.7) and D1 short — a Dutch mean-reversion would transmit into an already-stressed Taiwan.
connections.why_non_obvious: D2 alone says 'outran neighbors'. Only joining the surprise surface (deep inflation miss, not a growth beat), the family-rank surface (unanimous last-place across four families), and the trade graph (twin Germany contradicting) shows the rally has no cross-surface support and even has a downstream conduit to Taiwan.
connections.falsifiable_check: Event study: for D2 short flags ≥2.5σ where the country simultaneously ranks in the bottom decile of ≥3 signal families AND has ECO_INFL_SURPRISE_Z < -3, test 21d forward return vs. D2-only flags. Falsified if the conditioned set shows no additional negative drift.

connections.title: Indonesia: every model surface screams long while flows, price and curve scream stop
connections.mechanism: Indonesia is rank 1 in leadlag (1/15), rank 1 in twins (1/34), rank 2 in graph_twohop — the strongest multi-family long lean in the packet. But etf_contra ranks it 33/33 (contrarian family says most-crowded long), ETF_FLOW_21D_Z is +3.21 (largest inflow) yet 1m return is -9.07% (worst in universe), and SOV_2S10S_Z252 is -2.2 (curve flattening at a 1y extreme). Money is pouring in and the market is still falling, into a flattening curve, while its largest regional demand anchor ChinaA (-7.53% 1m, ChinaA-Vietnam/ASEAN trade channel) drags. This is either the best-in-class buy-the-gap setup or a value trap where flows are catching a falling knife.
connections.why_non_obvious: Each family in isolation would go long Indonesia; only joining the flow surface (+3.21z inflow), the return surface (-9.07%) and the sovereign curve extreme reveals the model consensus is the crowded side of the trade, not the informed side.
connections.falsifiable_check: Backtest: countries with top-3 composite long rank across ≥3 families but bottom-2 etf_contra rank and ETF_FLOW_21D_Z > +2.5 alongside 1m return < -8%. Falsified if 21-63d forward returns for this cohort are indistinguishable from the unconditional top-3 family-rank cohort.

connections.title: Energy +45% 3m makes the Japan D1 short a three-surface pincer via yen tail pricing
connections.mechanism: Energy index +44.9% 3m is the packet's biggest commodity impulse. Japan, a near-total energy importer, has D1 short (-2.44, 6d persisting: ToT deteriorating, equity+REER not repriced), FX_BF25_Z252 at +2.81 (options market paying up for JPY tail wings — stress pricing, not carry complacency), and SOV_2S10S_Z252 at +2.1 (the ONLY steepening extreme in the universe, consistent with imported-inflation term premium). The G2 gap is marked 'repriced_against' at low tension (0.192), i.e., equities have shrugged. The mirror leg: Saudi Arabia holds a G2 long gap (tot_impulse rank 1/34) also 'repriced_against' — the same energy shock is unabsorbed on BOTH ends of the ToT transfer.
connections.why_non_obvious: The D1 detector sees only ToT-vs-price. The FX vol surface (butterfly extreme, not implied vol level) and the lone steepening sovereign curve show two independent markets already pricing the stress the equity market ignores — and the Saudi long leg pairs the identical shock symmetrically without any detector linking them.
connections.falsifiable_check: Event study on historical episodes where energy 3m return > +30%: measure 21-63d relative return of Japan vs Saudi Arabia equity, conditioned on JPY BF25 z > +2. Falsified if the importer-vs-exporter spread shows no negative drift for Japan relative to Saudi.

connections.title: Turkey: FX options say calm exactly while D1 and CPI revisions say squeeze
connections.mechanism: Turkey has the packet's most anomalous FX-vol cluster: FX_RR25_Z252 -2.7, FX_BF25_Z252 -2.23, FX_IMPVOL_Z252 -2.03 — risk reversals, wings and implied vol ALL at 1y lows, i.e. the options market is priced for unusual TRY calm. Simultaneously D1 has Turkey short at -2.18 for 7 days (longest-running ToT dislocation tonight) as energy (+44.9%) and fertilizers (+37.8%) — both major Turkish imports — rip. cpi_rev ranks Turkey 2/33, but with a +1.44% 1m equity return the local market prices neither leg. Compressed vol + persistent unpriced ToT shock is a coiled-spring configuration: if the ToT deterioration forces FX adjustment, hedges are at their cheapest exactly when the fundamental stress is at its longest duration.
connections.why_non_obvious: The FX-vol detector flags 'cheap vol' and D1 flags 'ToT gap' separately; the joint read — maximal fundamental stress duration coinciding with minimal option-implied stress — is the setup, and neither single-surface detector can see the contradiction.
connections.falsifiable_check: Historical study: episodes where a country has D1 short active ≥5 days AND FX_IMPVOL_Z252 < -1.5. Test whether subsequent 21d realized FX vol and equity drawdown exceed the unconditional D1 baseline. Falsified if compressed implied vol shows no incremental predictive power.

connections.title: US growth/PMI at -3.84σ plus inverted-curve extreme threatens the Taiwan/HK lead-lag chain
connections.mechanism: US complex prints -3.84σ growth AND PMI surprises with SOV_2S10S_Z252 at -2.6 (deepest flattening in the packet) across U.S., NASDAQ and US SmallCap. The four strongest lead-lag edges in the graph all run US→Taiwan/HK (NASDAQ→Taiwan 0.533, U.S.→Taiwan 0.522, SmallCap→HK 0.488, SmallCap→Taiwan 0.477). Taiwan is already carrying a new D9 flag (-2.7), a persisting D1 short (-2.34) and a fully-decayed G3 gap; Hong Kong is -8.57% 1m yet ranks 1/21 in graph_bank and 5/34 twins. The lead-lag surface says the US macro shock hasn't finished propagating — Taiwan's positive +0.98% 1m return sits directly downstream of a -3.84σ US growth miss with a ~1 period lag.
connections.why_non_obvious: No detector conditions the Taiwan/HK dislocations on the US surprise surface. The chain (US macro miss → lag-1 graph edges → follower markets whose own detectors are already flashing) is a two-hop join across three surfaces; Taiwan's mildly positive month makes it look calm on a single screen.
connections.falsifiable_check: Event study: US ECO_GROWTH_SURPRISE_Z < -3 episodes; measure Taiwan and Hong Kong 5-21d forward returns vs their unconditional distribution, and vs episodes where Taiwan has no concurrent D1/D9 flag. Falsified if follower returns show no incremental downside when local dislocations are co-active.

connections.title: Malaysia is the only ASEAN market with outflows — the contrarian long inside a crowded region
connections.mechanism: ASEAN flow extremes are lopsided: Indonesia +3.21z, Thailand +2.93z, Singapore +2.88z inflows — but Malaysia is -2.72z, the region's only large outflow, after -4.23% 1m. Yet Malaysia ranks 1/33 in etf_contra (the contrarian family's single strongest long), 4/34 twins and 5/30 graph_twohop, and shares the ChinaA trade complex (ChinaA-Vietnam 296.6bn corridor) with the inflow-heavy peers. Meanwhile Singapore and Thailand carry etf_contra ranks 30/33 and 31/33 (most crowded) with D1-adjacent G2 short gaps on Singapore. Within one region the flow surface and the contrarian family point to a rotation: capitulation leg (Malaysia) vs crowded legs (Singapore/Thailand/Indonesia).
connections.why_non_obvious: Regional flow z-scores are each flagged individually, but the intra-ASEAN cross-section — one -2.7z outflow surrounded by three +2.9z-plus inflows, with the contrarian family ranking them at exact opposite extremes — is a relative-value structure no single-country detector assembles.
connections.falsifiable_check: Backtest a regional pair: within a bloc, long the member with ETF_FLOW_21D_Z < -2 and top-3 etf_contra rank vs short members with flow z > +2.5 and bottom-5 etf_contra rank; measure 21-63d spread. Falsified if the spread has no positive mean vs a random intra-bloc pair.

connections.title: Philippines D2 short collides with rank-1 CPI revisions — momentum vs macro inside one market
connections.mechanism: Philippines is +8.27% 1m while every ASEAN neighbor bleeds (Indonesia -9.07%, Malaysia -4.23%, Thailand -2.0%) — D2 flags it short at -2.4 (4d persisting). But its family profile is internally contradictory: cpi_rev 1/33 and leadlag 2/15 (strong long leans) against twins 34/34, graph_twohop 29/30, graph_bank 20/21 (weakest structural ranks in the region). The graph families say its fundamentals-relative-to-twins cannot support a 17pp monthly gap over Indonesia; the CPI-revision family says local macro is genuinely improving. The resolution matters: either the neighbors converge up to the Philippines (Indonesia rank-1 twins long) or the Philippines converges down — the twins surface says both legs of that convergence at once.
connections.why_non_obvious: A single screen shows either 'Philippines strong momentum' or 'D2 overrun'. Joining the family ranks reveals the same market is simultaneously #1 in one family and #34 in another — and the twins family independently puts its divergence counterpart (Indonesia) at rank #1, making it a convergence-pair conjecture rather than a solo short.
connections.falsifiable_check: Test D2 short flags conditioned on twins rank in bottom-3: does the flagged country's 21d return vs its bloc average revert more than unconditional D2 flags? Run separately when the bloc's top twins-rank member had negative trailing 1m return. Falsified if no incremental convergence.

fable.note: Non-deterministic Fable synthesis over tonight's warehouse + graph. CONJECTURE only — the harness owns verdicts; nothing here is a trade.

Indonesia.thesis: A2 graph propagation: trade-weighted partner 21d returns exceed Indonesia by 12.0pp (z=+1.73 vs 3y) while Indonesia itself sits at -2.1z on 21d returns after the June 3-7 selloff. Mechanism: external demand shock pricing propagates from partners to the lagging endpoint; partners have already repriced upward. Wrong if the gap is Indonesia-specific stress: rupiah weakening past -1.5z on 5d FX strength, or D4 firing on Indonesia, invalidates the propagation read.

Hong Kong.thesis: A2 graph propagation: trade-weighted partners outran Hong Kong by 6.1pp on 21d (z=+2.09) with HK own 21d return at -1.7z. The China complex and regional partners have bounced from the June selloff while HK lags its network. Mechanism: HK is a high-beta conduit for the network it trades with; endpoint catch-up is the historical norm when the middle repriced first. Wrong if HK-specific factors (HIBOR squeeze, property credit) explain the lag - a D4 cross-asset flag on Hong Kong or 10Y/FX stress invalidates.

edge_board.selection_note: governance exception first; then gaps by LIVE tension, consensus extremes (≥3 families), fresh event windows, expiring theses — dedup by entity|direction, cap 5.
